ops.title: cc-context is a Go single-binary facade (ccx CLI + MCP) proxying swappable backend MCPs for compact codebase context
ops.body:
cc-context (the `ccx` CLI **and** a `cc-context` MCP server — **one Go static binary**) gives Claude compact codebase context behind a stable facade. Static `ccx_*` tools/commands proxy (sub-call) **swappable backend MCPs** through one routing table (`internal/router` is the single swap point): **semble** for NL search, **tilth** for symbol-search / file-outline / structural diff. Backends are an implementation detail — the model only ever sees the `cc-context` surface, so engines can be replaced without changing it. The CLI uses a fast subprocess transport; the MCP server holds persistent stdio child sessions. Distributed as a Claude Code plugin (`plugin/`, registered in the cc-skills marketplace) whose `bin/ccx` shim + `EnsureTilth` download-provision binaries on first use.

Enforcement is a **capt-hook `ccx` pack** (tiered hard block, with escape hatches): large unbounded `Read`, broad `git diff`, `sed -n` range, bare `cat <file>`, `ls -R`, and `find` enumeration are blocked → routed to ccx equivalents; `rg`/`grep` get warns.

**Why:** transcript mining (126M tool-output tokens) showed **`Read` = 71.6% of all tool-**output** tokens** — file reading is the lever, not `git diff` (~0.8%). (A later 100-session mining over **tool-IO chars** — a different denominator, inputs+outputs — found Bash ~39% / Read ~33% / Edit ~10%, with tool-IO ~68% of *all* chars; both agree tool I/O is the lever.) The rtk "token-compression illusion" critique is the north star: compress where tokens are (file reads, not shell output); never silently drop info (keep line numbers + a raw `--full` fallback); never claim "% saved" without a real benchmark. As of 2026-06, the `bench/ccxbench` benchmark was **redesigned away from cost-per-correct** to a **prompt high-water-mark token-savings** metric (paired `1 − H_ccx/H_base` on both-correct task pairs) plus **per-test trajectory attribution** (parse saved `results/<sess>/raw/*.json`, decompose the high-water into static_overhead/tool_result/history/hook_error/residual, auto-diagnose losses) and a deterministic Layer-1 `microbench` (ccx ≤ raw tokens per intent). Counts default to an **offline tiktoken proxy** (cl100k_base, no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) because the bench auths Claude only via spawnllm's Claude Code OAuth and the count-tokens endpoint needs an x-api-key (OAuth bearer 401s); the API stays an opt-in `count_fn` for a high-fidelity run. The proxy is sound here: the high-water headline comes from transcript usage (no tokenizer), and every count is used inside an arm-vs-arm ratio where systematic error cancels. Early finding: ccx can *lose* on small/haiku tasks by taking more turns + tool-calls, which the high-water + context-ledger now surface instead of hiding. Go chosen over Python for ~ms CLI startup + single-binary plugin `bin/` delivery, over Rust for glue simplicity (it's pure spawn/pipe/proxy/download). `uvx` stays a baseline assumption only because semble is Python (runs as a persistent facade child, off the hot path). Related: [[jj-prek-commit-corruption]].
